title: RepeaterBook API Integration (Retry)

requirements.text: Create a frontend service/utility module at frontend/src/services/repeaterBookApi.ts that calls the RepeaterBook public API at https://www.repeaterbook.com/api/export.php. The module must support querying by state (using the 'state_id' parameter with 2-letter state abbreviation), by city (using the 'city' parameter), and optionally by frequency. It must parse the JSON response and map RepeaterBook fields (Frequency, Offset, Uplink Tone, Downlink Tone, TSQ, Call Sign, Nearest City, State, Use, Operational Status, etc.) into the app's internal Repeater shape so it is compatible with existing RepeaterCard and RepeaterDetailModal components. Mark these entries with a source field set to 'repeaterbook' to distinguish them from locally submitted repeaters.

quotes: Yes go ahead and build the Repeaterbook API integration

acceptanceCriteria: Module exports a function (e.g., fetchRepeaterBookData) accepting state abbreviation, optional city, and optional frequency parameters
Function constructs correct query URL to https://www.repeaterbook.com/api/export.php with appropriate parameters
Response JSON is parsed and mapped to the app's internal Repeater shape
Returned objects include a source field set to 'repeaterbook'
Module handles network errors gracefully and returns an empty array on failure

requirements.text: Update the Directory Page (frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx) to integrate the RepeaterBook API. When a user selects a state or enters a city in the filter bar, query the RepeaterBook API for live repeater data in addition to the locally stored repeaters. Display a loading indicator while fetching. Show RepeaterBook results in the same RepeaterCard grid alongside local results. Include a visible source badge or label on each card indicating whether the data is from 'RepeaterBook' (live) or 'Local' (user-submitted). Both sources must coexist — do not remove or hide existing local repeater display.

quotes: Yes go ahead and build the Repeaterbook API integration

acceptanceCriteria: Selecting a state in the filter bar triggers a RepeaterBook API fetch for that state
Entering a city triggers a RepeaterBook API fetch for that city
A loading spinner or indicator is shown while the RepeaterBook API request is in-flight
RepeaterBook results render in the same RepeaterCard grid as local repeaters
Each card clearly displays a 'RepeaterBook' or 'Local' source badge
Local (user-submitted) repeaters continue to display alongside RepeaterBook results

requirements.text: Ensure the RepeaterDetailModal (frontend/src/components/RepeaterDetailModal.tsx) works correctly for both local repeaters and RepeaterBook-sourced repeaters. For RepeaterBook entries that lack certain fields, display 'N/A' for missing values. Disable the favorite button for RepeaterBook entries and show a tooltip explaining that favoriting is only available for locally submitted repeaters.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the detail modal for a RepeaterBook entry renders all available fields correctly
Missing fields on RepeaterBook entries display 'N/A' instead of blank or undefined values
The favorite button is visually disabled for RepeaterBook entries
A tooltip on the disabled favorite button explains that favoriting is only available for locally submitted repeaters
The favorite button remains fully functional for local repeater entries

requirements.text: Add a prominent informational banner on the Directory Page (frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx) stating that live ham radio repeater data is sourced from RepeaterBook.com, and that GMRS repeaters can be submitted manually via the submission form. Include a clickable link to https://www.repeaterbook.com that opens in a new tab. Style the banner consistent with the existing dark ham-radio theme using amber/orange accent colors.
acceptanceCriteria: An informational banner is displayed on the Directory Page above or below the filter bar
Banner text states that live ham radio data is sourced from RepeaterBook.com
Banner text states that GMRS repeaters can be submitted via the submission form
A link to https://www.repeaterbook.com is present and opens in a new tab
Banner styling matches the existing dark navy/charcoal ham-radio theme with amber/orange accents

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/ (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts) or frontend/src/main.tsx
Do not alter the existing backend Motoko actor or data model
Do not remove or break the existing local repeater display on the Directory Page
nonGoals: MyGMRS.com API integration (no public API available)
Backend changes to store RepeaterBook data persistently
Bulk import or seeding of RepeaterBook data into the local database
Any real-time or WebSocket-based data syncing
